# ramda.py

[![Build Status](https://github.com/slavaGanzin/ramda.py/actions/workflows/python-package.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/slavaGanzin/ramda.py/actions/workflows/python-package.yml)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/ramda.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/ramda)

Python Clone of [Ramda.js](http://ramdajs.com). Improved fork of [Jack Firth's original impementation](https://github.com/jackfirth/pyramda)

```sh
pip install ramda
```

```python
>>> from ramda import *
>>> inc(1)
2
>>> map(inc, [1, 2, 3])
[2, 3, 4]
>>> incEach = map(inc)
>>> incEach([1, 2, 3])
[2, 3, 4]
```

g(_, 2)(1)(3)
g(_, 2)(1, 3)
g(_, 2)(_, 3)(1)

Restricts a number to be within a range.
Also works for other ordered types such as Strings and Dates

functions.name: ramda.union_with.union_with
documentation:
Combines two lists into a set (i.e. no duplicates) composed of the elements
of each list. Duplication is determined according to the value returned by
applying the supplied predicate to two list elements

functions.name: ramda.keys.keys
documentation:
Returns a list containing the names of all the enumerable own properties of
the supplied object.
Note that the order of the output array is not guaranteed to be consistent
across different JS platforms

functions.name: ramda.compose.compose
documentation:
Performs right-to-left function composition. The rightmost function may have
any arity; the remaining functions must be unary.
Note: The result of compose is not automatically curried

functions.name: ramda.negate.negate
documentation: Negates its argument

functions.name: ramda.tap.tap
documentation:
Runs the given function with the supplied object, then returns the object.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given as second parameter

functions.name: ramda.gte.gte
documentation:
Returns true if the first argument is greater than or equal to the second;
false otherwise

functions.name: ramda.find.find
documentation:
Returns the first element of the list which matches the predicate, or
undefined if no element matches.
Dispatches to the find method of the second argument, if present.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.adjust.adjust
documentation:
Applies a function to the value at the given index of an array, returning a
new copy of the array with the element at the given index replaced with the
result of the function application

functions.name: ramda.find_last.find_last
documentation:
Returns the last element of the list which matches the predicate, or
undefined if no element matches.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.contains.contains
documentation:
Returns true if the specified value is equal, in R.equals
terms, to at least one element of the given list; false otherwise

functions.name: ramda.unapply.unapply
documentation:
Takes a function fn, which takes a single array argument, and returns a
function which:
takes any number of positional arguments;
passes these arguments to fn as an array; and
returns the result.
In other words, R.unapply derives a variadic function from a function which
takes an array. R.unapply is the inverse of R.apply

functions.name: ramda.where_eq.where_eq
documentation:
Takes a spec object and a test object; returns true if the test satisfies
the spec, false otherwise. An object satisfies the spec if, for each of the
spec's own properties, accessing that property of the object gives the same
value (in R.equals terms) as accessing that property of the
spec.
whereEq is a specialization of where

functions.name: ramda.filter.filter
documentation:
Takes a predicate and a Filterable, and returns a new filterable of the
same type containing the members of the given filterable which satisfy the
given predicate. Filterable objects include plain objects or any object
that has a filter method such as Array.
Dispatches to the filter method of the second argument, if present.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.from_pairs.from_pairs
documentation:
Creates a new object from a list key-value pairs. If a key appears in
multiple pairs, the rightmost pair is included in the object

functions.name: ramda.dissoc_path.dissoc_path
documentation:
Makes a shallow clone of an object, omitting the property at the given path.
Note that this copies and flattens prototype properties onto the new object
as well. All non-primitive properties are copied by reference

functions.name: ramda.flatten.flatten_until
documentation:
Returns a new list by pulling every item out of it (and all its sub-arrays)
and putting them in a new array, depth-first

functions.name: ramda.either.either
documentation:
A function wrapping calls to the two functions in an || operation,
returning the result of the first function if it is truth-y and the result
of the second function otherwise. Note that this is short-circuited,
meaning that the second function will not be invoked if the first returns a
truth-y value.
In addition to functions, R.either also accepts any fantasy-land compatible
applicative functor

functions.name: ramda.union.union
documentation:
Combines two lists into a set (i.e. no duplicates) composed of the elements
of each list

functions.name: ramda.reduce_while.reduce_while
documentation:
Like reduce, reduceWhile returns a single item by iterating
through the list, successively calling the iterator function. reduceWhile
also takes a predicate that is evaluated before each step. If the predicate
returns false, it "short-circuits" the iteration and returns the current
value of the accumulator

functions.name: ramda.median.median
documentation: Returns the median of the given list of numbers

functions.name: ramda.omit.omit
documentation: Returns a partial copy of an object omitting the keys specified

functions.name: ramda.intersperse.intersperse
documentation:
Creates a new list with the separator interposed between elements.
Dispatches to the intersperse method of the second argument, if present

functions.name: ramda.difference_with.difference_with
documentation:
Finds the set (i.e. no duplicates) of all elements in the first list not
contained in the second list. Duplication is determined according to the
value returned by applying the supplied predicate to two list elements

functions.name: ramda.until.until
documentation:
Takes a predicate, a transformation function, and an initial value,
and returns a value of the same type as the initial value.
It does so by applying the transformation until the predicate is satisfied,
at which point it returns the satisfactory value

functions.name: ramda.join.join
documentation:
Returns a string made by inserting the separator between each element and
concatenating all the elements into a single string

functions.name: ramda.project.project
documentation: Reasonable analog to SQL select statement

functions.name: ramda.lt.lt
documentation:
Returns true if the first argument is less than the second; false
otherwise

functions.name: ramda.evolve.evolve
documentation:
Creates a new object by recursively evolving a shallow copy of object,
according to the transformation functions. All non-primitive properties
are copied by reference.
A transformation function will not be invoked if its corresponding key
does not exist in the evolved object

functions.name: ramda.ap.ap
documentation:
ap applies a list of functions to a list of values.
Dispatches to the ap method of the second argument, if present. Also
treats curried functions as applicatives

functions.name: ramda.chain.chain
documentation:
chain maps a function over a list and concatenates the results. chain
is also known as flatMap in some libraries
Dispatches to the chain method of the second argument, if present,
according to the FantasyLand Chain spec

functions.name: ramda.split.split
documentation:
Splits a string into an array of strings based on the given
separator

functions.name: ramda.take.take
documentation:
Returns the first n elements of the given list, string, or
transducer/transformer (or object with a take method).
Dispatches to the take method of the second argument, if present

functions.name: ramda.aperture.aperture
documentation:
Returns a new list, composed of n-tuples of consecutive elements. If n is
greater than the length of the list, an empty list is returned.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.eq_by.eq_by
documentation:
Takes a function and two values in its domain and returns true if the
values map to the same value in the codomain; false otherwise

functions.name: ramda.has.has
documentation: Returns whether or not an object has an own property with the specified name

functions.name: ramda.not_func.not_func
documentation:
Creates a new list out of the two supplied by applying the function to each
equally-positioned pair in the lists. The returned list is truncated to the
length of the shorter of the two input lists

functions.name: ramda.apply.apply
documentation:
Applies function fn to the argument list args. This is useful for
creating a fixed-arity function from a variadic function. fn should be a
bound function if context is significant

functions.name: ramda.try_catch.try_catch
documentation:
tryCatch takes two functions, a tryer and a catcher. The returned
function evaluates the tryer; if it does not throw, it simply returns the
result. If the tryer does throw, the returned function evaluates the
catcher function and returns its result. Note that for effective
composition with this function, both the tryer and catcher functions
must return the same type of results

functions.name: ramda.subtract.subtract
documentation: Subtracts its second argument from its first argument

functions.name: ramda.pair.pair
documentation: Takes two arguments, fst and snd, and returns [fst, snd]

functions.name: ramda.nth.nth
documentation:
Returns the nth element of the given list or string. If n is negative the
element at index length + n is returned

functions.name: ramda.for_each_obj_indexed.for_each_obj_indexed
ramda.group_with.group_with
documentation:
Takes a list and returns a list of lists where each sublist's elements are
all satisfied pairwise comparison according to the provided function.
Only adjacent elements are passed to the comparison function

functions.name: ramda.reduce.reduce
documentation:
Returns a single item by iterating through the list, successively calling
the iterator function and passing it an accumulator value and the current
value from the array, and then passing the result to the next call.
The iterator function receives two values: (acc, value). It may use
R.reduced to shortcut the iteration.
The arguments' order of reduceRight's iterator function
is (value, acc).
Note: R.reduce does not skip deleted or unassigned indices (sparse
arrays), unlike the native Array.prototype.reduce method. For more details
on this behavior, see:
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/reduce#Description
Dispatches to the reduce method of the third argument, if present. When
doing so, it is up to the user to handle the R.reduced
shortcuting, as this is not implemented by reduce

functions.name: ramda.cons.cons
documentation:
Creates a new list out of the two supplied by applying the function to each
equally-positioned pair in the lists. The returned list is truncated to the
length of the shorter of the two input lists

functions.name: ramda.split_when.split_when
documentation:
Takes a list and a predicate and returns a pair of lists with the following properties:
the result of concatenating the two output lists is equivalent to the input list;
none of the elements of the first output list satisfies the predicate; and
if the second output list is non-empty, its first element satisfies the predicate

functions.name: ramda.path_satisfies.path_satisfies
documentation:
Returns true if the specified object property at given path satisfies the
given predicate; false otherwise

functions.name: ramda.is_nil.is_nil
documentation: Checks if the input value is null or undefined

functions.name: ramda.remove.remove
documentation:
Removes the sub-list of list starting at index start and containing
count elements. Note that this is not destructive: it returns a copy of
the list with the changes.
No lists have been harmed in the application of this function

functions.name: ramda.index_of.index_of
documentation:
Returns the position of the first occurrence of an item in an array, or -1
if the item is not included in the array. R.equals is used to
determine equality

functions.name: ramda.is_empty.is_empty
documentation:
Returns true if the given value is its type's empty value; false
otherwise

functions.name: ramda.without.without
documentation:
Returns a new list without values in the first argument.
R.equals is used to determine equality.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.replace.replace
documentation: Replace a substring or regex match in a string with a replacement

functions.name: ramda.modulo.modulo
documentation:
Divides the first parameter by the second and returns the remainder. Note
that this function preserves the JavaScript-style behavior for modulo. For
mathematical modulo see mathMod

functions.name: ramda.all_pass.all_pass
documentation:
Takes a list of predicates and returns a predicate that returns true for a
given list of arguments if every one of the provided predicates is satisfied
by those arguments.
The function returned is a curried function whose arity matches that of the
highest-arity predicate

functions.name: ramda.merge.merge
documentation:
Create a new object with the own properties of the first object merged with
the own properties of the second object. If a key exists in both objects,
the value from the second object will be used

functions.name: ramda.product.product
documentation: Multiplies together all the elements of a list

functions.name: ramda.intersection.intersection
documentation:
Combines two lists into a set (i.e. no duplicates) composed of those
elements common to both lists

functions.name: ramda.map_accum_right.map_accum_right
documentation:
The mapAccumRight function behaves like a combination of map and reduce; it
applies a function to each element of a list, passing an accumulating
parameter from right to left, and returning a final value of this
accumulator together with the new list.
Similar to mapAccum, except moves through the input list from
the right to the left.
The iterator function receives two arguments, value and acc, and should
return a tuple [value, acc]

functions.name: ramda.T.T
documentation: A function that always returns true. Any passed in parameters are ignored

functions.name: ramda.any.any
documentation:
Returns true if at least one of elements of the list match the predicate,
false otherwise.
Dispatches to the any method of the second argument, if present.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.range.range
documentation: Returns a list of numbers from from (inclusive) to to (exclusive)

functions.name: ramda.where.where
documentation:
Takes a spec object and a test object; returns true if the test satisfies
the spec. Each of the spec's own properties must be a predicate function.
Each predicate is applied to the value of the corresponding property of the
test object. where returns true if all the predicates return true, false
otherwise.
where is well suited to declaratively expressing constraints for other
functions such as filter and find

functions.name: ramda.to_pairs.to_pairs
documentation:
Converts an object into an array of key, value arrays. Only the object's
own properties are used.
Note that the order of the output array is not guaranteed to be consistent
across different JS platforms

functions.name: ramda.split_at.split_at
documentation: Splits a given list or string at a given index

functions.name: ramda.assoc_path.assoc_path
documentation:
Makes a shallow clone of an object, setting or overriding the nodes required
to create the given path, and placing the specific value at the tail end of
that path. Note that this copies and flattens prototype properties onto the
new object as well. All non-primitive properties are copied by reference

functions.name: ramda.juxt.juxt
documentation: juxt applies a list of functions to a list of values

functions.name: ramda.all.all
documentation:
Returns true if all elements of the list match the predicate, false if
there are any that don't.
Dispatches to the all method of the second argument, if present.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.slice.slice
documentation:
Returns the elements of the given list or string (or object with a slice
method) from fromIndex (inclusive) to toIndex (exclusive).
Dispatches to the slice method of the third argument, if present

functions.name: ramda.identity.identity
documentation:
A function that does nothing but return the parameter supplied to it. Good
as a default or placeholder function

functions.name: ramda.symmetric_difference.symmetric_difference
documentation:
Finds the set (i.e. no duplicates) of all elements contained in the first or
second list, but not both

functions.name: ramda.merge_with_key.merge_with_key
documentation:
Creates a new object with the own properties of the two provided objects. If
a key exists in both objects, the provided function is applied to the key
and the values associated with the key in each object, with the result being
used as the value associated with the key in the returned object

functions.name: ramda.unfold.unfold
documentation:
Builds a list from a seed value. Accepts an iterator function, which returns
either false to stop iteration or an array of length 2 containing the value
to add to the resulting list and the seed to be used in the next call to the
iterator function.
The iterator function receives one argument: (seed)

functions.name: ramda.mean.mean
documentation: Returns the mean of the given list of numbers

functions.name: ramda.max.max
documentation: Returns the larger of its two arguments

functions.name: ramda.find_last_index.find_last_index
documentation:
Returns the index of the last element of the list which matches the
predicate, or -1 if no element matches.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.partial.partial
documentation:
Takes a function f and a list of arguments, and returns a function g.
When applied, g returns the result of applying f to the arguments
provided initially followed by the arguments provided to g

functions.name: ramda.take_last.take_last
documentation:
Returns a new list containing the last n elements of the given list.
If n > list.length, returns a list of list.length elements

functions.name: ramda.once.once
documentation:
Accepts a function fn and returns a function that guards invocation of
fn such that fn can only ever be called once, no matter how many times
the returned function is invoked. The first value calculated is returned in
subsequent invocations

functions.name: ramda.drop_last.drop_last
documentation: Returns a list containing all but the last n elements of the given list

functions.name: ramda.difference.difference
documentation:
Finds the set (i.e. no duplicates) of all elements in the first list not
contained in the second list. Objects and Arrays are compared in terms of
value equality, not reference equality

functions.name: ramda.reject.reject
documentation:
The complement of filter.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position. Filterable
objects include plain objects or any object that has a filter method such
as Array

functions.name: ramda.to_upper.to_upper
documentation: The upper case version of a string

functions.name: ramda.flip.flip
documentation:
Returns a new function much like the supplied one, except that the first two
arguments' order is reversed

functions.name: ramda.map_obj_indexed.map_obj_indexed
documentation:
An Object-specific version of map. The function is applied to three
arguments: (value, key, obj). If only the value is significant, use
map instead

functions.name: ramda.append.append
documentation:
Returns a new list containing the contents of the given list, followed by
the given element

functions.name: ramda.nth_arg.nth_arg
documentation: Returns a function which returns its nth argument

functions.name: ramda.path_or.path_or
documentation:
If the given, non-null object has a value at the given path, returns the
value at that path. Otherwise returns the provided default value

functions.name: ramda.ascend.ascend
documentation:
Makes an ascending comparator function out of a function that returns a value
that can be compared with < and >

functions.name: ramda.head.head
documentation:
Returns the first element of the given list or string. In some libraries
this function is named first

functions.name: ramda.prop.prop
documentation:
Returns a function that when supplied an object returns the indicated
property of that object, if it exists

functions.name: ramda.map_accum.map_accum
documentation:
The mapAccum function behaves like a combination of map and reduce; it
applies a function to each element of a list, passing an accumulating
parameter from left to right, and returning a final value of this
accumulator together with the new list.
The iterator function receives two arguments, acc and value, and should
return a tuple [acc, value]

functions.name: ramda.count_by.count_by
documentation:
Counts the elements of a list according to how many match each value of a
key generated by the supplied function. Returns an object mapping the keys
produced by fn to the number of occurrences in the list. Note that all
keys are coerced to strings because of how JavaScript objects work.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.update.update
documentation:
Returns a new copy of the array with the element at the provided index
replaced with the given value

functions.name: ramda.empty.empty
documentation:
Returns the empty value of its argument's type. Ramda defines the empty
value of Array ([]), Object ({}), String (''), and Arguments. Other
types are supported if they define <Type>.empty,
<Type>.prototype.empty or implement the
FantasyLand Monoid spec.
Dispatches to the empty method of the first argument, if present

functions.name: ramda.prop_or.prop_or
documentation:
If the given, non-null object has an own property with the specified name,
returns the value of that property. Otherwise returns the provided default
value

functions.name: ramda.unless.unless
documentation:
Tests the final argument by passing it to the given predicate function. If
the predicate is not satisfied, the function will return the result of
calling the whenFalseFn function with the same argument. If the predicate
is satisfied, the argument is returned as is

functions.name: ramda.dec.dec
documentation: Decrements its argument

functions.name: ramda.inner_join.inner_join
documentation:
Takes a predicate pred, a list xs, and a list ys, and returns a list
xs' comprising each of the elements of xs which is equal to one or more
elements of ys according to pred.
pred must be a binary function expecting an element from each list.
xs, ys, and xs' are treated as sets, semantically, so ordering should
not be significant, but since xs' is ordered the implementation guarantees
that its values are in the same order as they appear in xs. Duplicates are
not removed, so xs' may contain duplicates if xs contains duplicates

functions.name: ramda.pick_by.pick_by
documentation:
Returns a partial copy of an object containing only the keys that satisfy
the supplied predicate

functions.name: ramda.or_func.or_func
documentation:
Creates a new list out of the two supplied by applying the function to each
equally-positioned pair in the lists. The returned list is truncated to the
length of the shorter of the two input lists

functions.name: ramda.unary.unary
documentation:
Wraps a function of any arity (including nullary) in a function that accepts
exactly 1 parameter. Any extraneous parameters will not be passed to the
supplied function

functions.name: ramda.divide.divide
documentation: Divides two numbers. Equivalent to a / b

functions.name: ramda.if_else.if_else
documentation:
Creates a function that will process either the onTrue or the onFalse
function depending upon the result of the condition predicate

functions.name: ramda.sum.sum
documentation: Adds together all the elements of a list

functions.name: ramda.invert_obj.invert_obj
documentation:
Returns a new object with the keys of the given object as values, and the
values of the given object, which are coerced to strings, as keys. Note
that the last key found is preferred when handling the same value

functions.name: ramda.sort_by.sort_by
documentation: Sorts the list according to the supplied function

functions.name: ramda.sort.sort
documentation:
Returns a copy of the list, sorted according to the comparator function,
which should accept two values at a time and return a negative number if the
first value is smaller, a positive number if it's larger, and zero if they
are equal. Please note that this is a copy of the list. It does not
modify the original

functions.name: ramda.props.props
documentation:
Acts as multiple prop: array of keys in, array of values out. Preserves
order

functions.name: ramda.gt.gt
documentation:
Returns true if the first argument is greater than the second; false
otherwise

functions.name: ramda.path_eq.path_eq
documentation:
Determines whether a nested path on an object has a specific value, in
R.equals terms. Most likely used to filter a list

functions.name: ramda.merge_all.merge_all
documentation: Merges a list of objects together into one object

functions.name: ramda.uniq.uniq
documentation:
Returns a new list containing only one copy of each element in the original
list. R.equals is used to determine equality

functions.name: ramda.path.path
documentation: Retrieve the value at a given path

functions.name: ramda.call.call
documentation:
Returns the result of calling its first argument with the remaining
arguments. This is occasionally useful as a converging function for
R.converge: the first branch can produce a function while the
remaining branches produce values to be passed to that function as its
arguments

functions.name: ramda.drop_repeats_with.drop_repeats_with
documentation:
Returns a new list without any consecutively repeating elements. Equality is
determined by applying the supplied predicate to each pair of consecutive elements. The
first element in a series of equal elements will be preserved.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.reduced.reduced
documentation:
Returns a value wrapped to indicate that it is the final value of the reduce
and transduce functions. The returned value should be considered a black
box: the internal structure is not guaranteed to be stable.
Note: this optimization is unavailable to functions not explicitly listed
above. For instance, it is not currently supported by
reduceRight

functions.name: ramda.split_every.split_every
documentation: Splits a collection into slices of the specified length

functions.name: ramda.group_by.group_by
documentation:
Splits a list into sub-lists stored in an object, based on the result of
calling a String-returning function on each element, and grouping the
results according to values returned.
Dispatches to the groupBy method of the second argument, if present.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.contains_with.contains_with
documentation:
Creates a new list out of the two supplied by applying the function to each
equally-positioned pair in the lists. The returned list is truncated to the
length of the shorter of the two input lists

functions.name: ramda.sort_with.sort_with
documentation: Sorts a list according to a list of comparators

functions.name: ramda.use_with.use_with
documentation:
Accepts a function fn and a list of transformer functions and returns a
new curried function. When the new function is invoked, it calls the
function fn with parameters consisting of the result of calling each
supplied handler on successive arguments to the new function.
If more arguments are passed to the returned function than transformer
functions, those arguments are passed directly to fn as additional
parameters. If you expect additional arguments that don't need to be
transformed, although you can ignore them, it's best to pass an identity
function so that the new function reports the correct arity

functions.name: ramda.concat.concat
documentation:
Returns the result of concatenating the given lists or strings.
Note: R.concat expects both arguments to be of the same type,
unlike the native Array.prototype.concat method. It will throw
an error if you concat an Array with a non-Array value.
Dispatches to the concat method of the first argument, if present.
Can also concatenate two members of a fantasy-land
compatible semigroup

functions.name: ramda.drop.drop
documentation:
Returns all but the first n elements of the given list, string, or
transducer/transformer (or object with a drop method).
Dispatches to the drop method of the second argument, if present

functions.name: ramda.and_func.and_func
documentation:
Creates a new list out of the two supplied by applying the function to each
equally-positioned pair in the lists. The returned list is truncated to the
length of the shorter of the two input lists

functions.name: ramda.zip.zip
documentation:
Creates a new list out of the two supplied by pairing up equally-positioned
items from both lists. The returned list is truncated to the length of the
shorter of the two input lists.
Note: zip is equivalent to zipWith(function(a, b) { return [a, b] })

functions.name: ramda.to_lower.to_lower
documentation: The lower case version of a string

functions.name: ramda.multiply.multiply
documentation: Multiplies two numbers. Equivalent to a * b but curried

functions.name: ramda.prepend.prepend
documentation:
Returns a new list with the given element at the front, followed by the
contents of the list

functions.name: ramda.starts_with.starts_with
documentation: Checks if a list starts with the provided values

functions.name: ramda.merge_with.merge_with
documentation:
Creates a new object with the own properties of the two provided objects. If
a key exists in both objects, the provided function is applied to the values
associated with the key in each object, with the result being used as the
value associated with the key in the returned object

functions.name: ramda.ends_with.ends_with
documentation: Checks if a list ends with the provided values

functions.name: ramda.reverse.reverse
documentation:
Returns a new list or string with the elements or characters in reverse
order

functions.name: ramda.prop_eq.prop_eq
documentation:
Returns true if the specified object property is equal, in
R.equals terms, to the given value; false otherwise.
You can test multiple properties with R.where

functions.name: ramda.drop_last_while.drop_last_while
documentation:
Returns a new list excluding all the tailing elements of a given list which
satisfy the supplied predicate function. It passes each value from the right
to the supplied predicate function, skipping elements until the predicate
function returns a falsy value. The predicate function is applied to one argument:
(value)

functions.name: ramda.index_by.index_by
documentation:
Given a function that generates a key, turns a list of objects into an
object indexing the objects by the given key. Note that if multiple
objects generate the same value for the indexing key only the last value
will be included in the generated object.
Acts as a transducer if a transformer is given in list position

functions.name: ramda.min_by.min_by
documentation:
Takes a function and two values, and returns whichever value produces the
smaller result when passed to the provided function

functions.name: ramda.lte.lte
documentation:
Returns true if the first argument is less than or equal to the second;
false otherwise

functions.name: ramda.inc.inc
documentation: Increments its argument

functions.name: ramda.any_pass.any_pass
documentation:
Takes a list of predicates and returns a predicate that returns true for a
given list of arguments if at least one of the provided predicates is
satisfied by those arguments.
The function returned is a curried function whose arity matches that of the
highest-arity predicate

functions.name: ramda.scan.scan
documentation:
Scan is similar to reduce, but returns a list of successively
reduced values from the left

functions.name: ramda.invert.invert
documentation:
Same as R.invertObj, however this accounts for objects with
duplicate values by putting the values into an array

functions.name: ramda.dissoc.dissoc
documentation: Returns a new object that does not contain a prop property

functions.name: ramda.comparator.comparator
documentation:
Makes a comparator function out of a function that reports whether the first
element is less than the second

functions.name: ramda.of.of
documentation:
Returns a singleton array containing the value provided.
Note this of is different from the ES6 of; See
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Array/of

functions.name: ramda.repeat.repeat
documentation: Returns a fixed list of size n containing a specified identical value
